content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and MMM.

Consider two infinite arrays AAA and BBB, where


Ai=max⁡(i,Ni)A_i = \max\left(i, \frac{N}{i}\right)Ai​=max(i,iN​) if iii divides NNN; and Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 otherwise.
Bi=max⁡(i,Mi)B_i = \max\left(i, \frac{M}{i}\right)Bi​=max(i,iM​) if iii divides MMM; and Bi=0B_i = 0Bi​=0 otherwise.
For example, if N=6N = 6N=6, then A=[6,3,3,0,0,6,0,0,0,…]A = [6, 3, 3, 0, 0, 6, 0, 0, 0, \ldots]A=[6,3,3,0,0,6,0,0,0,…]

You are standing on the 2D plane, at point (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1).

In one move, you can move in any of the four directions, as long as both coordinates remain positive. That is, from point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y), you can move to (x−1,y),(x+1,y),(x,y−1),(x-1, y), (x+1, y), (x, y-1),(x−1,y),(x+1,y),(x,y−1), or (x,y+1)(x, y+1)(x,y+1), as long as both coordinates of the destination cell are positive.

The cost of point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) is defined to be Ax×ByA_x\times B_yAx​×By​.

Find the minimum total cost of moving from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (P,Q)(P, Q)(P,Q), where the total cost of a path is the sum of costs of all points visited along the way (including the initial and final points).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing four space-separated integers N,M,P,N, M, P,N,M,P, and QQQ as described in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the minimum cost to reach (P,Q)(P, Q)(P,Q).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The arrays AAA and BBB are both [2,2,0,0,0,…][2, 2, 0, 0, 0, \ldots][2,2,0,0,0,…].
This is because, since N=2N = 2N=2, we have A1=max⁡(1,21)=2A_1 = \max(1, \frac{2}{1}) = 2A1​=max(1,12​)=2 and A2=max⁡(2,22)=2A_2 = \max(2, \frac{2}{2}) = 2A2​=max(2,22​)=2 while all following elements are 000. The same applies to array BBB.
One optimal path to reach (2,2)(2, 2)(2,2) is (1,1)→(1,2)→(2,2)(1, 1)\to (1, 2)\to (2, 2)(1,1)→(1,2)→(2,2), which has a cost of 2×2+2×2+2×2=122\times 2 + 2\times 2 + 2\times 2 = 122×2+2×2+2×2=12.
Test case 222: Every path from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (3,10)(3, 10)(3,10) has a cost of 444.
